name: Operations and Prevention Expansion Pack
description: Supplemental local records, insurance, visit, prevention, and follow-up workflow knowledge.
reviewStatus: local pack requires workflow and privacy review before production deployment

records.title: Visit Preparation and Doctor Question Packet
records.summary: Creates a pre-visit packet structure using symptom timeline, medicine list, readings, labs, main questions, and what changed since the last visit.
records.safetyNotes: Urgent symptoms should be handled first before preparing routine visit notes.

records.title: Discharge Medicine Reconciliation Checklist
records.summary: Supports discharge follow-up by listing new medicines, stopped medicines, changed instructions, next appointments, and warning signs to watch.
records.safetyNotes: Do not guess discharge medicine changes. Use the official discharge summary and contact the care team if details are unclear.

records.title: Insurance Appeal Evidence Checklist
records.summary: Builds a checklist for denial letters, provider notes, test results, prior authorization documents, service dates, and appeal deadlines.
records.safetyNotes: This organizes claim evidence and does not determine legal strategy or plan decisions.

records.title: Prior Authorization Call Note and Follow-up Workflow
records.summary: Organizes prior-auth follow-up using reference numbers, call date, representative name, missing documents, expected turnaround, and next action.
records.safetyNotes: Keep exact insurer and provider contact details factual and dated. Do not rely on memory for appeal or authorization deadlines.

records.title: Vaccine and Screening Calendar Planner
records.summary: Helps track routine prevention by organizing vaccine status, screening history, age, family history, chronic conditions, and next due items.
records.safetyNotes: Use the planner as a reminder structure. Exact screening decisions should still be confirmed with a clinician.

records.title: Diabetes Foot Care and Daily Skin Check
records.summary: Provides a daily foot-check structure for diabetes using skin changes, numbness, wounds, shoes, swelling, and when to contact a clinician.
records.safetyNotes: Open wounds, spreading redness, severe pain, black discoloration, or fever need prompt medical attention.

records.title: Home Fall-Prevention and Mobility Safety Check
records.summary: Builds a home-safety checklist using stairs, rugs, bathroom support, lighting, footwear, dizziness, and assistive-device routine.
records.safetyNotes: Head injury, new confusion, inability to stand, or repeated falls should be treated as direct medical assessment needs.

records.title: Travel Medicine Packing and Refill Checklist
records.summary: Organizes travel medicine preparation using refill timing, copies of prescriptions, storage needs, time-zone questions, and backup supply planning.
records.safetyNotes: Do not change medicine schedules across travel without checking official label instructions or clinician advice when needed.

records.title: Lab Trend Comparison and Repeat-Test Worksheet
records.summary: Creates a worksheet for comparing the latest result with prior dates, reference ranges, fasting status, symptoms, and follow-up questions.
records.safetyNotes: Do not decide that a change is safe or unsafe without the full clinical context and clinician review.

records.title: Caregiver Handoff and Symptom Watch List
records.summary: Supports caregiver handoff using current symptoms, medicines, readings, warning signs, next steps, and contact details in one local record.
records.safetyNotes: Caregiver notes should be factual, current, and updated quickly when symptoms change or urgent care is needed.
